heading: Welcome
text: course teaches practical workflow accelerometer-based research: planning data collection, programming downloading devices, organizing files, processing raw data, checking quality, cleaning outputs, creating analysis-ready dataset.course uses ActiGraph examples, GGIR R, Stata reproducible workflow. principles broader one device software package, every study document protocol analytical decisions.

heading: 0.6 Course workflow
text: Plan deploy → download → organize → process → inspect quality → clean standardize → prepare final dataset → document report.

text: accelerometer sensor records changes acceleration time, typically along three axes. human research, device records movement-related signals can processed interpretable summaries. raw signal commonly stored gravitational units (g) metres per second squared (m/s²).device directly “know” whether person exercising, sitting, asleep. heading: 1.2 Lesson 1.2: What can accelerometers measure?
text:
Accelerometer recordings can support estimates several dimensions movement behavior. exact outcome depends device, placement, algorithm, calibration, processing choices.
Examples potential accelerometer-derived measures.
Different monitor placements can useful different questions. example, thigh-worn monitor may better support posture classification, wrist placement often improves wear comfort continuous data collection. best placement follows research question protocol—universal rule.Video 1.2 — raw movement outcomes (target: 6–8 minutes)
Use diagram showing path signal → processing → quality control → outcomes. Include one concrete example, daily movement signal can contribute activity-volume estimate.

chapter: Module 4 Setting Up R and GGIR
heading: 4.2 Lesson 4.2: Understand the GGIR workflow
text:
GGIR organized sequential processing stages. broad terms, workflow handles raw-signal preparation calibration, data-quality non-wear processing, sleep-related analysis requested, generation summary outcomes.staged design allows later choices adjusted without necessarily repeating every earlier stage. useful inputs, arguments, outputs carefully documented.Video 4.2 — guided tour GGIR workflow (target: 8–10 minutes)
Use diagram explain sequence processing stages study-specific arguments decided.

chapter: Module 4 Setting Up R and GGIR

text: Create parameter sheet launching run. state device, wear location, protocol, time zone, expected wear schedule, analysis windows, thresholds algorithms selected study team.Critical review point: GGIR arguments—including non-wear, sleep, valid-data, intensity choices—must justified protocol. copy values another population placement without documented decision.

heading: Module 5 Checking Data Quality
text: Data quality control bridge automated processing defensible analysis dataset. identifies incomplete recordings, implausible patterns, technical errors, decisions need documented analysis.end module, able :Review file summaries identify records requiring follow-.Use visual inspection structured quality-control step.Maintain decision log exclusions, corrections, unresolved issues.

heading: 5.1 Lesson 5.1: Start with summaries
text:
Review processing outputs summarize calibration, wear non-wear information, recording duration, quality indicators selected project. Look records missing expected output, unusual values, require protocol-based decision.remove records value looks surprising. First verify source file, processing log, protocol, relevant deployment notes.Video 5.1 — Reading processing summaries (target: 6–8 minutes)
Show locate approved quality-control outputs flag records review.

heading: 5.2 Lesson 5.2: Visual inspection
text:
Daily time-series plots can reveal patterns single summary number hides: long gaps, unusual signal shapes, abrupt changes, recordings match expected schedule.Visual inspection use consistent protocol. Record reviewed, reason flag, evidence checked, final decision.Video 5.2 — Structured visual inspection (target: 7–9 minutes)
Use de-identified example plots demonstrate flag record document reason.

heading: 5.3 Lesson 5.3: Decision logging
text: Create quality-control log study identifier, issue category, evidence reviewed, decision, reviewer, date, follow-action. Keep log processing outputs.

heading: 5.3.1 Optional learning activity
text: three fictional records, write one quality-control note : one acceptable record, one record needing follow-, one record excluded using stated protocol rule.

heading: 5.4 Wrap-up

heading: 6.1 Lesson 6.1: Understand the output level
text:
output files contain one row per participant; others contain one row per participant-day time segment. cleaning, confirm unit observation every file choose dataset matches research question.common error merge collapse files understanding observation level. Always check identifiers, dates, expected number rows first.Video 6.1 — Reading participant-level day-level outputs (target: 6–8 minutes)

heading: 6.2 Lesson 6.2: Standardize variables

Build data dictionary states original variable name, standardized name, definition, unit, source file, transformation, missing-value rule. Keep variables essential later quality checks, even part primary outcome set.Video 6.2 — Creating cleaning plan data dictionary (target: 6–8 minutes)
Demonstrate simple dictionary script-based transformation rather manual spreadsheet edits.
